title: The viewer's look and colors
question: How should the viewer look — keep the current styling, or refine it toward a calm Apple-like style with a custom color scheme?
rationale: Refine the whole look toward Apple's calm style — system font, whitespace, hairline dividers, soft corners, segmented controls, card-like panels — with a deep-teal accent on true-grey and a coordinated phase-color family. It's styling polish only: the structure (d2), stage grouping (d6), and calm detail (d9) stay, in one offline file.

history.from: A persistent translucent top bar holding the project name on the left and the theme/help buttons on the right
history.reason: Removed the top bar for calmer chrome — theme and help buttons float at the top of the page, and the name moved into the hero with an optional app-icon lockup (shown only when an icon file is available) to its left

history.from: Detail pop-up: chosen option, why, and question packed into one tinted box with uniform faint labels
history.reason: Restyled the detail pop-up to the same Apple-calm system — a phase chip, the question as a framing deck, a light answer card, the rationale as its own readable prose block, and legible eyebrow labels with hairline rhythm. Carries the palette and restraint here; the layout change itself is recorded on d9.

options.label: Apple-style polish, deep-teal accent on true-grey

tradeoffs.note: CSS/JS changes in the generator; no new dependencies
uses the built-in system font, no downloaded font or framework
neutral-heavy with one accent reads calm in any light
the teal accent needs a contrast check in both light and dark
a coordinated family tuned for enough hue separation to still tell apart

options.label: Keep the current styling (leave as is)

tradeoffs.note: no unified palette or Apple-style restraint
busier; works against the calm-review goal (d9)
